title: Frames-Within-Frames Design
breadcrumbs: Design Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Frames-Within-Frames Design Components give access to the user view of a business activity. A crucial part of the design of a Uniface component is the frames-within-frames diagram. Access path analysis requires a frames-within-frames definition for the Uniface component under consideration. A frames-within-frames diagram shows the entities that are accessed as entity frames painted inside other entity frames. The sequence in which the frames are nested defines the order in which the component accesses the entities, starting with the outermost entity. The definition reflects the access profile of the component. It helps in answering the following questions: Does the component implement the required user view? Is the component susceptible to performance bottle-necks? The following illustrations show an example of the frames-within-frames diagram and the access profile it is based on. Figure 1.
